name: Salas Reservoir Loop from Muiños
name_original: Muiños – vuelta por el embalse del río Salas
description: This moderate 19.1-mile mountain biking route circles the scenic embalse de Salas within the Xurés-Gêrés Biosphere Reserve.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Muíños features a landscape characterized by river valleys, reservoirs, and forested hills within the Geres-Xures Biosphere Reserve. The region offers varied terrain suitable for different skill levels, from moderate paths around water bodies to challenging ascents. Elevation changes are common, providing diverse mountain bike trails Muíños difficulty. Best mountain bike trails around Muíños The most popular mountain bike trail is Lobios – vuelta por el río Lima y Minas das Sombras – Geres-Xures Biosphere Reserve, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural reserve and river area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Salas Reservoir Loop from Muiños, a moderate 19.1 miles (30.7 km) path. This route offers views of the Salas Reservoir and surrounding countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Rio Salas Bridge – Dolmen of Casola do Foxo loop from Igrexa de Santa Comba de Bande, a 32.5 miles (52.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain including a river bridge and historical sites, often completed in about 5 hours 1 minute. Mountain biking around Muíños is defined by its reservoirs, river valleys, and forested hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy mountain bike trails Muíños for beginners and challenging mountain bike trails Muíños advanced riders. The routes in Muíños are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Muíños's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Muíños?
A: The mountain biking trails around Muíños are characterized by diverse terrain, including river valleys, large reservoirs, and forested hills. You'll encounter varied surfaces and elevation changes, offering everything from moderate paths around water bodies to challenging ascents within the Geres-Xures Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Muíños?
A: Yes, Muíños offers options for different ability levels, including easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners. Out of the 16 available routes, 2 are specifically rated as easy, providing a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What is a good moderate mountain bike trail for an intermediate rider in Muíños?
A: For intermediate riders looking for a moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993771103/salas-reservoir-loop-from-muinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas Reservoir Loop from Muiños&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 30.7 km (19.1 miles) route offers scenic views of the Salas Reservoir and the surrounding countryside.

Q: Which mountain bike trails in Muíños are considered challenging for advanced riders?
A: Advanced riders seeking a challenge will find several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993983546/lobios-vuelta-por-el-rio-lima-y-minas-das-sombras-geres-xures-biosphere-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lobios – vuelta por el río Lima y Minas das Sombras – Geres-Xures Biosphere Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, a 41.0 km (25.5 miles) trail that explores the natural reserve and river area with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Muíños?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Muíños are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19879696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Salas Bridge – Dolmen of Casola do Foxo loop from Igrexa de Santa Comba de Bande&lt;/a&gt; is a 52.4 km (32.5 miles) loop that takes you through varied terrain and historical sites.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Muíños?
A: The trails often pass by significant natural and historical attractions. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Casola do Foxo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3112491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquis Querquennis Roman Camp&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5278288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Baths of Bande&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its hot springs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Caldo Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; and waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6066651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pitões das Júnias Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Muíños?
A: The mountain bike trails in Muíños are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the reservoirs and river valleys, and the options available for different skill levels.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Muíños?
A: The longest mountain bike trail in Muíños is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6951544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muíños loop from Campamento Romano de Aquis Querquennis&lt;/a&gt;. This challenging route spans 67.2 km (41.8 miles) and features significant elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced riders.

Q: Are there any trails that combine natural beauty with historical sites?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19879696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Salas Bridge – Dolmen of Casola do Foxo loop from Igrexa de Santa Comba de Bande&lt;/a&gt; is a great example. This route not only offers beautiful river views but also passes by the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Casola do Foxo&lt;/a&gt;, providing a blend of natural and historical exploration.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in the Muíños area?
A: There are 16 mountain bike trails documented around Muíños, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. This includes 2 easy, 7 moderate, and 7 difficult routes.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The region is defined by its reservoirs, and many trails offer stunning views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993771103/salas-reservoir-loop-from-muinos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas Reservoir Loop from Muiños&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing expansive vistas of the Salas Reservoir throughout the ride.

Q: Is there a mountain bike route that explores the Geres-Xures Biosphere Reserve?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993983546/lobios-vuelta-por-el-rio-lima-y-minas-das-sombras-geres-xures-biosphere-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lobios – vuelta por el río Lima y Minas das Sombras – Geres-Xures Biosphere Reserve&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 41.0 km (25.5 miles) trail specifically designed to explore the natural beauty and protected areas within the Geres-Xures Biosphere Reserve.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Muiños – vuelta por el embalse del río Salas mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: This route is generally rated as moderate for mountain biking. While much of the trail is accessible, it features varied terrain, including forest tracks, and some technical sections like the Regato de Lobeira. Riders should have good fitness and some advanced riding skills for certain parts, as technical difficulty can vary.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical of a mountainous natural environment. You will encounter forest tracks and potentially wet river sections. Some parts may feature rocky and uneven ground, requiring good bike handling skills. The route circles the Salas Reservoir, offering a mix of surfaces.

faqs.question: Is the Muiños – vuelta por el embalse del río Salas a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Muiños – vuelta por el embalse del río Salas' is a loop trail. It circles the scenic Salas Reservoir, bringing you back to your starting point.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: As you ride, you'll enjoy picturesque views of the Salas Reservoir Park. The trail also passes by the ancient Dolmen of Casola do Foxo and crosses the Rio Salas Bridge. The entire route is nestled within the Xurés-Gêrés Transboundary Biosphere Reserve, offering stunning natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Muiños is a known BTT Center, suggesting designated parking facilities for cyclists are available. It's advisable to look for parking near the main access points to the Salas Reservoir area in Muiños.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Muiños – vuelta por el embalse del río Salas trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be pleasant, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter rides are possible but may involve colder, potentially wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Muiños – vuelta por el embalse del río Salas trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Xurés-Gêrés Transboundary Biosphere Reserve, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice responsible pet ownership and clean up after your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to mountain bike in the Xurés-Gêrés Transboundary Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no general entrance fees to access the Xurés-Gêrés Transboundary Biosphere Reserve for activities like mountain biking. However, specific regulations may apply to certain areas or activities, especially regarding protected zones. It's always a good idea to check local guidelines before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this mountain biking route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other local paths. You will find yourself riding along parts of the Camiño de Cados, Camiño de Fontelo, and Camiño do Foxo for short distances.

faqs.question: What makes this trail unique compared to other mountain biking routes in the region?
faqs.answer: This trail stands out due to its scenic loop around the artificial Salas Reservoir, offering continuous water views within the stunning Xurés-Gêrés Transboundary Biosphere Reserve. It combines natural beauty with varied terrain, including historical sites like the Dolmen of Casola do Foxo, making it a diverse and engaging ride within a well-established BTT Center.
